2026-04-23T02:09:28.8702177Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8702717Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8703142Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8703680Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8703989Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8704219Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8704383Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8704557Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8704734Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8704964Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8705162Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8705460Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T02:09:28.8705717Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-23T02:11:41.6494301Z Filed issue **[Extensions] Docs: Update README for Azure.Identity → Azure.Core migration in 1.14.0** for the `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` package. The 1.14.0 release dropped `Azure.Identity` as a dependency (types moved into `Azure.Core`), but the README still implies users need `Azure.Identity` and is missing the standard **Prerequisites**, **Key concepts**, **Troubleshooting**, and **Next steps** sections. 2026-04-23T02:11:45.3673458Z ◆ Let me analyze what changed:
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3673678Z   
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3673930Z   1. **Microsoft.Extensions.Azure 1.14.0** - The CHANGELOG says:
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3674471Z      - "Adopted the new `Azure.Core` version that includes the identity types moved from `Azure.Identity`."
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3674922Z      
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3675755Z   2. The README still mentions `Azure.Identity` types like `ClientSecretCredential`, `EnvironmentCredential` in the code snippets but doesn't mention the behavior change where `Azure.Identity` types are now included in `Azure.Core` 1.53+.
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3676668Z   
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3676969Z   Let me look at the README more closely to understand what documentation gaps exist:
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3677343Z   
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3677513Z   The README currently:
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3678134Z   - Uses `ClientSecretCredential` and `EnvironmentCredential` in code snippets (from Azure.Identity)
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3678593Z   
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3678741Z   
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3679647Z   - Has the core sections like Getting started, Install, Register clients, Inject clients, Create named instances, Register custom client factory, and Contributing, but is missing standard documentation sections like Key concepts, Troubleshooting, and Next steps
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3681188Z   - Doesn't explain that the 1.14.0 release removed the `Azure.Identity` dependency in favor of `Azure.Core`, so the credential types in the examples now come from `Azure.Core` instead of a separate package
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3682734Z   - The README still shows code using credential types like `ClientSecretCredential` and `EnvironmentCredential` without clarifying they're now part of `Azure.Core`, which could confuse users about whether they still need to install `Azure.Identity` separately
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3684058Z   - Missing a Prerequisites section that would clarify the package dependencies after this change
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3684498Z   
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3685456Z   The biggest issue is that this release made a significant dependency shift, but the README doesn't document it clearly. Users won't understand that `Azure.Identity` types have moved into `Azure.Core` and that they might not need a separate `Azure.Identity` package anymore.
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3686521Z   
2026-04-23T02:11:45.3688000Z   I should also note the README is missing several standard sections like Key concepts, Troubleshooting, and Next steps—it jumps straight from Getting Started into Install the package and Register clients without that typical structure. I'll file an issue ... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2149 chars]

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. 2026-04-23T02:11:45.5988053Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T02:10:39.140503504Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Azure client library integration for ASP.NET Core\n\nMicrosoft.Extensions.Azure provides shared primitives to integrate Azure clients with ASP.NET Core [dependency injection][dependency_injection] and [configuration][configuration] systems.\n\n[Source code][source_root] | [Package (NuGet)][package]\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nInstall the ASP.NET Core integration library using [NuGet][nuget]:\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Microsoft.Extensions.Azure\n```\n\n### Register clients\n\nMake a call to `AddAzureClients` in your app's `ConfigureServices` method. You can use the provided builder to register client instances with your dependency injection container.\n\n```C# Snippet:ConfigureServices\n// Registering policy to use in ConfigureDefaults later\nbuilder.Services.AddSingleton\u003cDependencyInjectionEnabledPolicy\u003e();\n\nbuilder.Services.AddAzureClients(azureBuilder =\u003e {\n    // Register blob service client and initialize it using the KeyVault section of configuration\n    azureBuilder.AddSecretClient(builder.Configuration.GetSection(\"KeyVault\"))\n        // Set the name for this client registration\n        .WithName(\"NamedBlobClient\")\n        // Set the credential for this client registration\n        .WithCredential(new ClientSecretCredential(\"\u003ctenant_id\u003e\", \"\u003cclient_id\u003e\", \"\u003cclient_secret\u003e\"))\n        // Configure the client options\n        .ConfigureOptions(options =\u003e options.Retry.MaxRetries = 10);\n\n    // Adds a secret client using the provided endpoint and default credential set later\n    azureBuilder.AddSecretClient(new Uri(\"http://my.keyvault.com\"));\n\n    // Configures environment credential to be used by default for all clients that require TokenCredential\n    // and doesn't override it on per registration level\n    azureBuilder.UseCredential(new EnvironmentCredential());\n\n    // This would use configuration for auth and client settings\n    azureBuilder.ConfigureDefaults(builder.Configuration.GetSection(\"Default\"));\n\n    // Configure global retry mode\n    azureBuilder.ConfigureDefaults(options =\u003e options.Retry.Mode = RetryMode.Exponential);\n\n    // Advanced configure global defaults\n    azureBuilder.ConfigureDefaults((options, provider) =\u003e options.AddPolicy(provider.GetService\u003cDependencyInjectionEnabledPolicy\u003e(), HttpPipelinePosition.PerCall));\n\n    // Register blob service client and initialize it using the Storage section of configuration\n    azureBuilder.AddBlobServiceClient(builder.Configuration.GetSection(\"Storage\"))\n            .WithVersion(BlobClientOptions.ServiceVersion.V2019_02_02);\n});\n```\n\n### Inject clients\n\nTo use the client request the client type from any place that supports Dependency Injection (constructors, Configure calls, `@inject` razor definitions etc.)\n\n```C# Snippet:Inject\nvar secretClient = app.Services.GetRequiredService\u003cSecretClient\u003e();\nvar blobClientFactory = app.Services.GetRequiredService\u003cIAzureClientFactory\u003cBlobServiceClient\u003e\u003e();\n```\n\n### Create named instances\n\nIf client is registered as a named client inject `IAzureClientFactory\u003cT\u003e` and call `CreateClient` passing the name:\n\n```C# Snippet:ResolveNamed\nBlobServiceClient blobServiceClient = blobClientFactory.CreateClient(\"NamedBlobClient\");\n```\n\nConfiguration file used in the sample above:\n\n``` json\n{\n  \"Logging\": {\n    \"LogLevel\": {\n      \"Default\": \"Debug\"\n    }\n  },\n  \"AllowedHosts\": \"*\",\n  \"Default\": {\n    \"ClientId\": \"\u003cclient_id\u003e\",\n    \"ClientSecret\": \"\u003cclient_secret\u003e\",\n    \"TenantId\": \"\u003ctenant_id\u003e\",\n\n    \"TelemetryPolicy\": {\n      \"ApplicationId\": \"AppId\"\n    }\n  },\n  \"KeyVault\": {\n    \"VaultUri\": \"\u003cvault_uri\u003e\"\n  },\n  \"Storage\": {\n    \"serviceUri\": \"\u003cservice_uri\u003e\",\n    \"credential\": {\n      \"accountName\": \"\u003caccount_name\u003e\",\n      \"accountKey\": \"\u003caccount_key\u003e\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Registering a custom client factory\n\nIf you want to take control over how the client instance is created or need to use other dependencies during the client construction use the `AddClient\u003cTClient, TOptions\u003e` method.\n\nHere's and example of how to use `IOptions\u003cT\u003e` instance to construct the client:\n\n```C# Snippet:UsingOptionsForClientConstruction\npublic class MyApplicationOptions\n{\n    public Uri KeyVaultEndpoint { get; set; }\n}\n\npublic void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services)\n{\n    // Configure a custom options instance\n    services.Configure\u003cMyApplicationOptions\u003e(options =\u003e options.KeyVaultEndpoint = new Uri(\"http://localhost/\"));\n\n    services.AddAzureClients(builder =\u003e\n    {\n        // Register a client using MyApplicationOptions to get constructor parameters\n        builder.AddClient\u003cSecretClient, SecretClientOptions\u003e((options, credential, provider) =\u003e\n        {\n            var appOptions = provider.GetService\u003cIOptions\u003cMyApplicationOptions\u003e\u003e();\n            return new SecretClient(appOptions.Value.KeyVaultEndpoint, credential, options);\n        });\n    });\n}\n```\n\n## Contributing\nThis project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions require you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring that you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use your contribution. For details, visit https://cla.microsoft.com.\n\nWhen you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine whether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately (e.g., label, comment). Simply follow the instructions provided by the bot. You will only need to do this once across all repos using our CLA.\n\nThis project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][code_of_conduct]. For more information see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][code_of_conduct_faq] or contact opencode@microsoft.com with any additional questions or comments.\n\n\n\u003c!-- LINKS --\u003e\n[source_root]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/src\n[nuget]: https://www.nuget.org/\n[package]: https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/\n[configuration]: https://learn.microsoft.com/aspnet/core/fundamentals/configuration/?view=aspnetcore-3.0\n[dependency_injection]: https://learn.microsoft.com/aspnet/core/fundamentals/dependency-injection?view=aspnetcore-3.0\n[code_of_conduct]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/\n[code_of_conduct_faq]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/README.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}
2026-04-23T02:11:45.6028494Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T02:10:39.180061964Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Release History\n\n## 1.14.0 (2026-04-22)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Adopted the new `Azure.Core` version that includes the identity types moved from `Azure.Identity`.\n\n## 1.13.1 (2025-11-19)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated `Azure.Identity` dependency to version 1.17.1\n\n## 1.13.0 (2025-09-22)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated the Azure.Identity dependency to version 1.16.0\n\n## 1.12.0 (2025-06-12)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Added support for [managed identity as a federated identity credential](https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/workload-id/workload-identity-federation-config-app-trust-managed-identity?tabs=microsoft-entra-admin-center%2Cdotnet#azure-identity-client-libraries) in the client factory by specifying configuration item `credential` as \"managedidentityasfederatedidentity\" and providing the following named configuration items:\n\n  - `tenantId` : The tenant where the target resource was created\n  - `clientId` : The client identifier for the application, which must be granted access on the target resource\n  - One of [`managedIdentityClientId`, `managedIdentityObjectId`, `managedIdentityResourceId`] : The user-assigned managed identity which you configured as a Federated Identity Credential (FIC)\n  - `azureCloud`: One of the following Azure cloud environments:\n    - `public` for Entra ID Global cloud\n    - `usgov` for Entra ID US Government\n    - `china` for Entra ID China operated by 21Vianet\n\n\n## 1.11.0 (2025-03-15)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Added support for [AzurePipelinesCredential](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.identity.azurepipelinescredential?view=azure-dotnet) in the client factory by specifying configuration item `credential` as \"azurepipelines\" and providing each credential parameter as a named configuration item.\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Fixed an issue when creating clients from configuration using the ASP.NET Core integration testing library, `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.Testing`.  Due to a difference in how an section value is represented, logic was interpreting a setting with a child object as an empty connection string value.  The child object is now properly parsed and applied for client construction.  ([#48368](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/48368))\n\n## 1.10.0 (2025-02-11)\n\n### Other Changes\n- Updated dependencies to ensure they are up-to-date with the latest targets and trimming from built-in dependencies.\n\n## 1.9.0 (2024-11-26)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated dependency `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Abstractions` to version `8.0.2`\n- Updated dependency `Microsoft.Bcl.AsyncInterfaces` to version `8.0.0`\n\n## 1.8.0 (2024-11-05)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Added support for constructing a `ManagedIdentityCredential` from config by setting the `managedIdentityObjectId` key.\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated dependency `Azure.Identity` to version `1.13.1`.\n\n## 1.7.6 (2024-10-04)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Improved the efficiency of `AzureEventSourceLogForwarder` by eliminating message formatting. ([#46202](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/46202))\n\n## 1.7.5 (2024-08-15)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated dependency `Azure.Core` to version `1.42.0`.\n\n## 1.7.4 (2024-06-13)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated dependency `Azure.Identity` to version `1.11.4`.\n\n## 1.7.3 (2024-04-16)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated dependency `Azure.Identity` to version `1.11.0`.\n\n## 1.7.2 (2024-02-12)\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Fix several issues related to ThreadPool starvation for synchronous scenarios\n\n## 1.7.1 (2023-10-27)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated dependency `Azure.Identity` to version `1.10.3`.\n\n## 1.7.0 (2023-08-08)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Added support for creating `WorkloadIdentityCredential` objects from the configuration using the `\"credential\": \"workloadidentity\"`. Users must provide values for the `tenantId`, `clientId`, and newly added `tokenFilePath` keys in the configuration, or they must set the environment variables `AZURE_TENANT_ID`, `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`, and `AZURE_FEDERATED_TOKEN_FILE`.\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Updated dependency `Azure.Identity` to version `1.9.0`.\n\n## 1.6.3 (2023-03-10)\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Upgraded dependent `Azure.Core` to `1.30.0`.\n\n## 1.6.2 (2023-03-07)\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Added support for clients to be disposed via `IDisposable` or `IAsyncDisposable` when the service factory is disposed.\n- Changed tracking for client initialization to ensure that behavior is correct for value types registered as clients.\n\n## 1.6.0 (2022-10-12)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Support passing a semi-colon delimited list of additional tenants via the `additional
